name: Ohio Audiologist License
description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Ohio Speech and Hearing Professionals Board (SHPB)

address.streetAddress: 77 S. High St., Suite 1659, Columbus, OH 43215
address.description: Ohio Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Board, 77 South High Street, Suite 1642, Columbus, Ohio 43215-6108

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Ohio audiologist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.
Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Ohio have reciprocity for audiologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Ohio participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Ohio without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Ohio?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
